chunks.title: Introduction to Toxins
chunks.summary: The podcast opens with a discussion on the growing concern over toxins present in everyday products, emphasizing the cumulative nature of these chemicals and their potential negative impacts on human health. The host encourages empowerment through knowledge and the importance of making informed choices.
chunks.transcript: repeatedly what I find when I look into these chemicals and environmental exposures is that it's not if the chemical is toxic it's just a matter of time until enough studies are done that it's proven that it's toxic on this week's podcast I wanted to talk about toxins in your home I got an amazing comment on YouTube and I wanted to walk you through a day and think about the places during your day in which you are encountering phthalates parabens pfas BPA BPS fragrances endocrine disruptors things that are problematic for humans this podcast is meant to be empowering knowledge is power and to help you make changes in the podcast I will offer tools and solutions as we move through and I will offer resources for you to research the products that you are using and make informed decisions I think that overall humans are becoming less healthy or less fertile or less strong or more obese we're less happy and we're more chronically ill and I think a lot of this has to do with food and water but certainly there's a major contribution from environmental toxins and so understanding where you're getting exposed to these and eliminating them is critical to living the life that you were born to live this is your Birthright so there's a fun podcast to do and you guys enjoy it leave me a comment subscribe like and I'll do more like

chunks.title: Toxin Exposure in Daily Life
chunks.summary: The speaker outlines how individuals are exposed to environmental toxins from the moment they wake up, identifying common household items that may contain harmful substances. This includes bedding, personal care products, and cleaning agents.
chunks.transcript: I wanted to do a podcast on toxins in a home and this podcast is really inspired by a comment I got from one of you guys on YouTube uh Johnny the goat says I would love a podcast about healthy alternatives and solutions to everyday items that are harming us laundry items soaps shampoos Dental Care storage items fragrances deodorants Etc I feel like there's a lot of harm to be done by the chemicals and ingredients in these products but not enough common knowledge of why that is or how to combat it we'll put the comment up on the screen for you guys on YouTube thanks to Johnny for the comment it was a good inspiration for this podcast so I'll say from the outside of this podcast that this one is going to seem at the outset like Doom and Gloom because many of us essentially live in a toxic soup we live our lives from the moment we wake up and I want to do this podcast chronologically and it may end up being a two-part podcast but from the moment you wake up in your bed think about what your sheets are made of what the clothes you're wearing are made of are there fragrances on your sheets and then you get out of bed and you go to the bathroom maybe you have a bowel movement like the toilet paper you're using does that have things in it what about feminine care products what about tampons what about maxi pads what about Cosmetics what about the water you're brushing your teeth with what about your floss what about your toothpaste what about your perfumes what about your deodorant what about your body wash your shampoo anyway you guys get the idea all of those things have lots of chemicals that can affect us negatively as humans and so it all starts to feel a little bit overwhelming so as we start the podcast just take a deep breath and realize that I'm going to try to zoom out multiple times during this podcast to give you the 15 000 foot View and I'm going to offer Solutions and things you can do to change those items in your home and I think that any step is a step in the right direction you don't have to change everything all at once but that understanding that these are cumulative many of these chemicals phthalates parabens pfas which is perifluoro alkyl substances BPS bpe BPA so many of these substances they're cumulative and so the less you have in your life I think the better you're going to be overall so this isn't meant to be Doom and Gloom this isn't meant to be go into the woods and live in a animal skin it's meant to be how can you be aware of these chemicals that are literally everywhere in your life from the moment you wake up so the moment you go to sleep and how can you replace them how

chunks.title: Impact of Water Quality
chunks.summary: The importance of safe drinking water is highlighted, with an emphasis on the need for filtration systems to reduce harmful chemicals found in tap water. The host shares personal practices regarding water consumption and filtration.
chunks.transcript: that this will have a overall positive impact in your life because these are chemicals that are impacting us negatively as humans a lot of these chemicals we don't fully understand what they're doing so is it possible that some of them are benign yes is it possible that some of them are much worse than benign frankly harmful for humans either causing cancer reproductive illness hormonal disruption infertility growth delay in infants or women who are pregnant autoimmune diseases mental health issues yes those are all possible we're only beginning to understand this but there's a lot of let's just say legal wrangling and a special wording on labels and I think that a lot of This research may not actually get done to the level or degree that it needs to be so my perspective is have as much caution as you can in your life avoid these chemicals as much as possible and I think that you will lead a better quality life most of what I talk about in my work is the food that you put in your mouth so I've done previous episodes on water water quality I'm not really going to go into water quality today other than to say that the environmental working group has a list of tap water qualities across the country you can look at the city you live in and surely it is going to have 10 to 20 to 30 chemicals in it I would not drink tap water no matter where you are and I would consider some sort of filtration I've done this on a previous podcast I just use a reverse osmosis filter because it's simple and it gets

chunks.title: Animal-Based Diet Overview
chunks.summary: The host discusses the benefits of an animal-based diet, including organ meats, grass-fed meats, and raw dairy, as a foundation for good nutrition. The conversation touches on how this diet can lead to improvements in various health conditions.

chunks.title: Toxic Chemicals and Fertility
chunks.summary: Citing various studies, the podcast delves into the current fertility crisis and how environmental toxins, particularly those disrupting hormonal pathways, may contribute to declining infertility rates in both men and women.
